hero_049.earlyGame: Utilize Skill 2's magic circle to clear minion waves quickly while securing vision within the area for safe positioning. It deals 50% extra damage to non-hero units, allowing for efficient farming. Your movement speed increases while inside the circle, so use it to your advantage for positioning. To trigger the true damage from your Passive, ensure you land basic attacks and skills within 1.5-second intervals to gain an advantage in early small-scale skirmishes.
hero_049.midGame: You can place up to two magic circles simultaneously, enhancing your lane control. Place the 4 magic barriers from Skill 1 in pathways to prevent enemies from approaching, or to stun them for 0.75 seconds and assist teammates in securing kills. Enemies will not be stunned again if they touch the barriers consecutively, but their movement speed will be reduced, so it's effective to place them to cut off their escape routes. Combine this with the true damage from your Passive for high burst damage.
hero_049.lateGame: Skill 3's 2.2-second suppression becomes extremely powerful. You play the role of reliably locking down troublesome enemy assassins and carries, allowing your team to focus and eliminate them. If you fully channel Skill 3, a magic domain is generated at your feet, dealing additional magic damage and a 0.75-second stun, so try to complete the channel whenever possible. The true damage from your Passive also triggers every 1.2 seconds, providing high sustained damage.
hero_049.teamfight: Place Skill 1's barriers on the enemies' path to restrict their movement and control the teamfight with stuns and movement speed reduction. Lay down Skill 2's circle on the frontline to deal continuous damage and increase your movement speed, then use Skill 3 to suppress and completely neutralize the core enemy hero for 2.2 seconds. Maintain the consecutive attack state of your Passive using Skills 1 and 2, and contribute as your team's damage source by dealing continuous true damage.
hero_049.commonMistakes: If you move and cancel the channel during Skill 3's suppression, the magic domain that deals additional stun and damage won't be generated, and the lockdown time will be wasted. Additionally, failing to understand that Skill 1's stun doesn't stack on the same enemy and overlapping the barriers is inefficient. Another common mistake is wasting Skill 2 on empty space instead of enemy minions, failing to utilize the 50% extra damage to non-heroes.
hero_049.strengths: The greatest strength is the extremely powerful single-target suppression skill lasting up to 2.2 seconds, allowing you to reliably neutralize specific enemies. Moreover, the high minion clearing ability and vision from Skill 2, the area zoning and stun from Skill 1, and the continuous application of true damage from the Passive allow you to deal effective damage even to highly defensive tanks. The mobility gained from the movement speed increase within Skill 2's area is also excellent.
hero_049.weaknesses: During the powerful suppression of Skill 3, you cannot move and become defenseless, making you vulnerable to being focused by other enemies while channeling. Furthermore, Skill 1's stun cannot stack on the same enemy, so you cannot stun multiple enemies for a long duration. To deal the true damage from the Passive, you must continuously land attacks within 1.5-second intervals, which becomes difficult against highly mobile enemies who frequently distance themselves.

hero_050.earlyGame: Utilize the enhanced basic attack prepared every 5 seconds to knock back enemies and recover energy while progressing through the laning phase. Throw Skill 2's fan to deal magic damage and reduce movement speed from a distance, while lowering the enemy's magic defense to harass them. Utilize the rolling movement from the Passive, triggered by inputting a direction immediately after a skill or enhanced basic attack, to dodge enemy attacks, secure a safe position, and manage your energy.
hero_050.midGame: After reducing the enemy's magic defense with Skill 2, aim for kills with a combo using Skill 1's dash to knock up enemies in the area for 0.75 seconds. Since energy is recovered upon skill hits, it's crucial to land skills accurately to prevent energy depletion. Utilize the high mobility from the Passive's rolling to quickly roam to other lanes, and support your team's kills with Skill 1's knock-up or the enhanced basic attack's knockback.
hero_050.lateGame: Take on the role of disrupting the enemy formation with the powerful dash and knockback of Skill 3. It reduces the enemy's physical attack by 20% for 2.5 seconds, making it highly effective against physical carries. Lower the frontline's magic defense with Skill 2, and utilize its effect of dealing damage to enemies behind them to chip away at the backline. Continuously chain skills and Passive movement to employ hit-and-run tactics, exploiting enemy openings and fighting while recovering energy.
hero_050.teamfight: Spam Skill 2 from a distance to lower the enemy's magic defense and spread damage to enemies in the back. When you spot an opening, dash in with Skill 1 to knock up multiple enemies for 0.75 seconds, then use Skill 3 to knock them back, reducing their physical attack by 20% and dividing their formation. By combining the energy recovery from each skill hit and the disengage from the Passive's rolling, continuously deal damage while toying with the enemies.
hero_050.commonMistakes: A typical mistake is missing skills and failing to recover energy, leading to energy depletion where you cannot cast subsequent skills. Also, misjudging the direction of the Passive's rolling movement and diving straight into the enemy or under their tower is common. Skill 2 has the characteristic of dealing damage to enemies behind the target, but if you position yourself to only deal single-target damage without utilizing this, your poke efficiency drops significantly.
hero_050.strengths: Possesses abundant CC, including Skill 1's knock-up, Skill 3's knockback, and the enhanced basic attack's knockback. In addition to the magic defense reduction and powerful long-range poke capability of Skill 2, the ability to roll after every skill or enhanced attack grants extremely high mobility and evasion. Furthermore, Skill 3 applies a debuff that reduces the enemy's physical attack, providing outstanding burst damage and disruption against single targets.
hero_050.weaknesses: Casting skills consumes energy, and if you don't hit enemies to gain sufficient recovery, the vulnerability from missing a skill is immense. Additionally, failing to control the Passive's rolling movement risks moving into careless positions. While possessing powerful CC and mobility, the lack of durability-enhancing skills makes them very fragile; getting caught by enemy CC will result in being burst down instantly.

hero_051.earlyGame: Enter water areas (like the river) or generate water yourself with Skill 2 to enter Mermaid Mode, utilizing the HP/Mana regen per second and 50% reduction of movement speed slow effects to sustain in the lane. Mermaid Mode's basic attacks deal area damage, helping with minion clearing. Release Skill 1's sound waves to deal damage up to 4 times and reduce movement speed to harass, and cast it from within the water to knock back and stun enemies for favorable trades.
hero_051.midGame: Use Skill 2 to create water areas while healing allied heroes, supporting your whole team to receive the same HP and Mana regen effects as the mermaid diva. The healing amount and the water area's effects can significantly increase your team's sustained combat ability. Form a contract with a specific ally using Mermaid Gift, and use Skill 3 to reset their most powerful skill with the longest cooldown, creating an overwhelming advantage in skirmishes.
hero_051.lateGame: Your main job is to use Skill 3 to reset the powerful skills of allies that are key to teamfights. Prioritize your contracted partner to reset their skill, as allies can recast the skill within 5 seconds without consuming Mana, generating game-ending destructive power. Using Skill 2 in water areas reduces its CD by 25%, so utilize the river or your own water areas to frequently provide healing and water deployment to support the team.
hero_051.teamfight: Generate a water area in the center of your allies with Skill 2 to provide AoE healing and continuous HP/Mana regeneration. While inside the water, Skill 1's final wave transforms into a powerful CC that knocks back and stuns enemies, preventing enemy approaches and disrupting their formation. Then, use Skill 3 at the most crucial timing to instantly reset an ally's key skill. You also share the kill rewards from the reset skill, contributing to the team's overall growth.
hero_051.commonMistakes: A frequent mistake is casting Skill 1 outside of water areas, failing to obtain the crucial knockback and stun CC effects. Also, misunderstanding that Skill 3's cooldown depends on and lengthens based on the CD of the ally's reset skill, and using it carelessly to unnecessarily prolong your own CD is an issue. Since the benefit diminishes if the ally doesn't use the reset skill within 5 seconds, casting it when uncoordinated should also be avoided.
hero_051.strengths: Skill 3, which can completely reset the cooldown of an ally's powerful skill, is the greatest and most unique strength. Additionally, the generation of water areas with Skill 2 provides continuous HP and Mana regen not just for yourself but for the entire team, boasting exceptionally high support and sustain capabilities. Skill 1 transforming into an AoE knockback and stun within water is also excellent as a powerful self-defense and disruption tool, showing unparalleled strength in fights within water areas like the river.
hero_051.weaknesses: Since Skill 3's cooldown heavily depends on the ally's skill CD, its true value can only be shown immediately after an ally uses a powerful skill, making dependency on allies' actions very high. Furthermore, the powerful CC of Skill 1's knockback and stun requires being inside a water area to activate, making location dependency a weakness. Lacking skills to deal high damage yourself, the combat capability when alone is extremely low.

hero_052.earlyGame: Utilize Skill 2's dash, which deals 50% extra damage to monsters, and its HP recovery effect upon hitting enemy units to quickly clear the jungle while maintaining high health. Apply the hawk mark with Skill 1, and trigger it with other damage to deal additional physical damage equal to 6% of max HP, while also reducing CD and recovering Mana, to farm efficiently. The enhanced basic attack from the Passive after landing a skill also contributes to clearing speed.
hero_052.midGame: If out of combat for 4 seconds, your Passive increases movement speed by 10%, and casting Skill 3 increases movement speed by 50%, allowing you to rapidly traverse the map and launch ambushes. While flying, you can leap over terrain once, allowing approaches from unexpected routes. Skill 3's charge reduces the enemy's damage dealt by 30% for 3 seconds and lowers their movement speed, enabling you to finish them off with the Skill 1 and enhanced basic attack combo without giving them a chance to counterattack.
hero_052.lateGame: As bonus physical attack increases, the max HP percentage damage of Skill 1's mark rises, granting you extremely high burst damage. Use the overwhelming mobility of Skill 3 to dive directly into the enemy backline, applying the mark with Skill 1, dashing with Skill 2, and unleashing the enhanced basic attack for hit-and-run tactics. Since the HP recovery from hitting heroes with Skill 2 also scales with attack power, you can maintain survivability while dealing damage to multiple enemies.
hero_052.teamfight: Instead of charging straight into the teamfight, utilize Skill 3's flying state and terrain crossing to ambush fragile enemy attackers from the flank or rear. By landing Skill 3's charge on the enemy's main damage dealers, you also play a crucial role in reducing their damage dealt by 30% for 3 seconds, minimizing damage to your team. After securing a kill by applying the mark with Skill 1, use the recovery and movement of Skill 2's dash to quickly disengage to a safe position and wait for another opportunity to re-engage.
hero_052.commonMistakes: Failing to trigger the hawk mark applied by Skill 1 with another attack causes you to miss out on significant percentage damage, Mana recovery, and CD reduction, drastically lowering your burst damage. Also, failing to properly weave the Passive's enhanced basic attack into your skill combo often results in insufficient total damage. Reckless charges into the middle of the enemy team using Skill 3's flying state, only to get CC'd and killed without doing anything, should also be avoided.
hero_052.strengths: The greatest strength is the mobility to quickly traverse the entire map and ambush enemies, utilizing the overwhelming movement speed and terrain-crossing ability of Skill 3 and the Passive. The instant burst damage is extremely high due to Skill 1's mark dealing max HP percentage damage with high physical scaling, and Skill 3's charge makes you strong in duels by reducing the enemy's damage dealt by 30%. Furthermore, the HP recovery and extra monster damage from Skill 2 provide excellent jungle clear speed and sustain.
hero_052.weaknesses: If caught by enemies with powerful CC (crowd control), you will be killed quickly as your durability is not high. Also, the skill kit is specialized for approaching and dealing damage, lacking reliable disengage skills once you dive in, posing the risk of being stranded deep within the enemy formation. Mana consumption is intense, and failing to properly trigger Skill 1's mark to recover Mana will quickly lead to Mana depletion.

hero_053.earlyGame: Make full use of the Passive's drawing slash, utilizing its long reach to damage and slow enemies from a safe distance. Landing hits with the tip of the attack range triggers additional damage and heals your HP, making it crucial to maintain the distance for tip hits. Skill 1 jumps backward while attacking, allowing you to deal damage while dodging enemy skills, and if it hits an enemy, you can immediately cast the second phase, demonstrating mobility and burst damage from the early game.
hero_053.midGame: Continuously land Skill 2's swift slashes on enemies. Since hitting enemies with the tip of this skill also stuns them for 0.75 seconds, chain it from the slow effect of the Passive's drawing slash for reliable crowd control. By landing the second phase of Skill 1 near the starting point of the first phase, it becomes an Enhanced Swallow Return, dealing damage in a wider area, reducing Skill 3's CD, and finishing enemies with additional damage based on their missing HP.
hero_053.lateGame: Skill 3's 4-hit combo slash is your main damage source and lifeline. Since it heals a percentage of your HP each time it hits an enemy hero, it boasts high survivability even in teamfights. Capitalize on the mechanic where dealing damage with skills reduces the Passive's CD, continuously weaving skills and drawing slashes in combat. Adjust your positioning with Skill 1 and accurately snipe key enemy heroes with the tip stun of Skill 2.
hero_053.teamfight: Instead of fighting front-to-back, use your high mobility to flank from the sides or rear. Stun the enemy key targets with the tip of Skill 2, and immediately unleash the combo slash of Skill 3 to deal massive damage and heal yourself. Even if faced with counterattacks or being surrounded, use Skill 1's backward jump to deal damage while quickly disengaging, adopting a hit-and-run tactic by continuously harassing from the tip range of the Passive's drawing slash again.
hero_053.commonMistakes: The most significant mistake is getting too close to enemies, wasting the crucial effects like additional damage and stun, as both the Passive's drawing slash and Skill 2's stun effect only activate when hit at the tip of the attack range. Also, since Skill 1 jumps backward, you must input the stick in the opposite direction of where you want to move; operational errors often cause players to dive into the center of the enemy team or fail to escape.
hero_053.strengths: The Passive and Skill 2 have powerful bonuses for hitting with the tip, providing high mid-range harassment and control capabilities. Combining the double movement/evasion capability of Skill 1 with the high HP recovery upon hitting enemy heroes with Skill 3 grants immense potential to win one-sidedly while dodging enemy attacks. Since every skill hit reduces the Passive's cooldown, it also excels at outputting sustained mid-range damage.
hero_053.weaknesses: The base durability is set low, and Skill 3's healing effect doesn't activate unless it hits enemies, so getting stopped by powerful CC will result in instant death. Maximizing the skills' power constantly requires maintaining the precise distance of 'tip hits,' making the operation difficulty and reliance on player skill extremely high. Moreover, since Skill 1's movement direction is unique, operational errors in split-second decisions can be fatal.

hero_054.earlyGame: Capitalize on the continuous HP regeneration effect every 2 seconds from the Passive to stay in the lane longer and farm advantageously. When subjected to movement speed reductions, the healing amount increases further, allowing you to position aggressively without fearing enemy harassment. Activate Skill 2 to deal continuous physical damage to the surroundings, clearing minions quickly while approaching enemy heroes for favorable damage trades.
hero_054.midGame: Cast Skill 1 to increase movement speed by 30%, utilizing your mobility to actively rotate and help other lanes. The enhanced basic attack from Skill 1 features a dash toward the enemy and a 1.25-second silence effect, allowing you to seal the enemy's skill casts and attack without allowing a counterattack. It also applies a 5-second mark to the target, enabling follow-up damage as magic damage equal to 1% of max HP, so coordinate focus with allies to secure kills.
hero_054.lateGame: Utilize the powerful percentage magic damage of Skill 3 (16% of the enemy's max HP) to deal massive damage even to enemy frontline tanks. Skill 3 jumps to a targeted enemy, knocking them up for 0.5 seconds, and summons a holy seal that deals continuous damage to the surroundings for 5 seconds, excelling as the initiator of teamfights. Disrupt the enemy formation using the silence of Skill 1, the continuous damage of Skill 2, and the percentage damage and CC of Skill 3.
hero_054.teamfight: Use Skill 1 to increase movement speed and charge at backline heroes heavily reliant on skills, like mages and marksmen, neutralizing them with a 1.25-second silence. Immediately activate Skill 2 to scatter damage to all surrounding enemies, and slam Skill 3 onto the most troublesome enemy to inflict a knock-up and percentage damage. Near enemies marked by Skill 1, allies' movement speed also increases by 10%, strongly supporting the entire team's pursuit.
hero_054.commonMistakes: Skill 1's silence effect is most effective against heroes highly dependent on skills; wasting it on basic-attack-focused tanks diminishes its value. Also, a common mistake is hoarding Skill 3 too much, failing to realize that since its percentage magic damage relies on the enemy's max HP, using it early or mid-fight on enemies with high HP yields greater total damage than using it as a finishing blow on enemies with little HP remaining.
hero_054.strengths: The greatest strength is the overwhelming lane sustain capability from the unconditional continuous HP regeneration of the Passive (doubled when slowed). The movement speed increase and targeted dash + silence effect from Skill 1 are very easy to use, reliably hindering enemy actions. Additionally, possessing percentage magic damage of 16% max HP from Skill 3 allows for effective damage against tough tanks stacking physical defense, making the hero highly adaptable despite simple controls.
hero_054.weaknesses: Being a melee hero who must get close to deal damage, there is a weakness of being whittled down without being able to do anything against enemies who excel at kiting from afar or team comps with strong CC that restrict movement. Moreover, lacking defensive skills that instantly negate enemy attacks makes them prone to being focused and killed if they dive too deep into the enemy formation. The lack of a dedicated escape skill is also a drawback.

hero_055.earlyGame: Maximize the Passive where casting a skill enhances the next basic attack, making you charge at the enemy with a guaranteed critical hit. Since you have a 20% critical rate from the start, alternate between skills and enhanced basic attacks to aim for high burst damage. The charge from Skill 2 deals true damage upon touching monsters and has an instant kill effect on monsters below 15% HP, allowing for extremely fast jungle clearing.
hero_055.midGame: Utilize your high mobility and burst damage to launch ambushes across lanes. When approaching enemies, use Skill 2, taking advantage of the extra jump upon hitting the target and a 90% movement speed increase to instantly close the gap. When the enemy is about to counterattack with skills, cast Skill 1 to block their skills for 1.5 seconds, gaining 0.2 seconds of invincibility and a powerful shield, allowing you to deal damage one-sidedly and secure kills.
hero_055.lateGame: As you complete your equipment, the critical damage of the Passive's enhanced basic attack skyrockets, gaining the destructive power to instantly assassinate the enemy backline. Use Skill 3 to slam the Ruyi Jingu Bang into the ground, knocking up enemies in the area for 1 second and applying 3 stacks of marks simultaneously. Hitting enemies with these marks with basic attacks triggers additional physical damage, allowing massive damage against even tanky enemy heroes through consecutive attacks after Skill 3.
hero_055.teamfight: Charging straight into a teamfight will result in being focused down, so flank from the sides or behind after your team initiates, targeting the most fragile enemy attackers. Quickly close the gap with Skill 2, seal the enemy's actions with the area knock-up of Skill 3, and unleash the enhanced basic attacks from your Passive. If powerful enemy skills fly towards you, immediately block them with Skill 1 to gain a shield and invincibility, surviving while disengaging.
hero_055.commonMistakes: A very common mistake is inputting skills too rapidly in succession, forgetting to weave the Passive's enhanced basic attacks in between. This drastically lowers the total damage output. Additionally, while Skill 1 blocks an enemy 'skill' once, it does not trigger against basic attacks. Using it pointlessly against marksmen's basic attacks, or failing to block, not receiving the shield, and dying due to misjudgment are noticeable errors.
hero_055.strengths: The enhanced basic attack after a skill guarantees a critical hit and causes a charge towards the target, boasting overwhelming instant burst damage and pursuit capabilities. Since Skill 1 can nullify enemy skills to gain a shield and invincibility, the defensive adaptability is high despite being an assassin. Furthermore, the additional true damage and instant kill effect on monsters from Skill 2 make the jungle clear speed and objective control abilities exceptionally high.
hero_055.weaknesses: If you fail to block an enemy skill with Skill 1, the very low durability results in extreme fragility, leading to quick deaths. Also, since most of the damage relies on the enhanced basic attacks from the Passive, it is hard to output damage against enemies with skills that nullify or evade basic attacks. As the initial critical damage is capped at 150%, it takes time to complete items and output sufficient burst damage.

hero_056.earlyGame: Thanks to the Passive granting a constant 100 physical and magic defense to yourself and one ally in range, you are very tanky and can fight advantageously from the early game. In isolated situations with no allies nearby, your own defense increases by an additional 50%, making you strong in solo lane duels. Land the tap cast of Skill 1 to deal damage and reduce enemy movement speed while healing your HP, and utilize the 50% CD reduction effect to cycle the skill frequently.
hero_056.midGame: Utilize the hold cast of Skill 1. Throw your weapon in a designated direction to create a continuous damage area, reducing the enemies' physical defense by 50 for 2 seconds to boost your burst damage. By walking over to pick up the weapon yourself, you slash surrounding enemies, knocking them back and dealing extra damage. To secure escaping enemies, approach with the dash of Skill 2 to knock up enemies in the path for 0.75 seconds, then completely catch them with the subsequent massive movement speed reduction.
hero_056.lateGame: The powerful area control of Skill 3 is the key to teamfights. After a short charge, sunder the earth, dealing massive damage and knocking up enemies in the area for 1 second, instantly interrupting enemy skill casts. Furthermore, the resulting fissure remains for 5 seconds, continuously dealing damage every 0.5 seconds and applying a 30% movement speed reduction to enemies within it, playing a role in splitting the enemy formation in two and heavily restricting movement.
hero_056.teamfight: Stand on the frontline utilizing the defense increase from the Passive, protecting the ally hero with the lowest HP while fighting. Dive into the enemy team with Skill 2 to apply a knock-up, and immediately cast Skill 3 to knock up multiple enemies at once and interrupt the channeling of powerful enemy skills. Aim to divide enemies and restrict movement with the generated fissure, throw your weapon with the hold cast of Skill 1 to lower physical defense, and manipulate enemy positions with the knockback when picking it up.
hero_056.commonMistakes: Picking up the weapon thrown by holding Skill 1 triggers a powerful AoE attack and knockback, but a common mistake is getting too fixated on picking it up and becoming isolated deep within the enemy formation. Also, Skill 3 has a short charge time before casting, so firing it without predicting enemy movement makes it easy to dodge. Since the Passive's defense boost is distributed to allies when they are nearby, fighting recklessly and getting killed is another risk.
hero_056.strengths: The constant physical and magic defense increase from the Passive boasts high durability from the early game without relying on items. Possesses highly versatile and powerful CC and debuffs, including healing and CD reduction from tapping Skill 1, defense reduction and knockback from holding it, dash knock-up from Skill 2, and wide-area knock-up, continuous slow, and channel interruption from Skill 3. The ability to maintain the frontline while restricting enemy actions is extremely high.
hero_056.weaknesses: Since skill animations and casts (especially Skill 3's charge) are generally slow, it is difficult to land skills against highly mobile enemies who dart around quickly. Additionally, as their durability relies on the Passive's defense increase, the advantage is greatly diminished if enemies build defense penetration items or have true damage. After throwing the weapon by holding Skill 1, the tap effect cannot be used until it's picked up, creating a vulnerability.

hero_057.earlyGame: Take advantage of the Passive's shotgun characteristic by getting as close to the enemy as possible to hit them with all 4 bullets for maximum damage. Every time a bullet hits an enemy hero, physical armor penetration stacks up to 12 times, making consecutive attacks at close range extremely powerful. Use Skill 1 to increase movement speed by 40% and make the next basic attack a double shot, instantly unleashing burst damage on jungle monsters or enemy heroes.
hero_057.midGame: Utilize the dash and knockback of Skill 2 for ambushes. Since hitting an enemy hero with the dash reduces Skill 2's cooldown by 2 seconds, aggressively collide with enemies to disrupt their actions while creating a point-blank distance. Rapidly approach with the movement speed boost from Skill 1, block their escape by knocking them back with Skill 2, and adopt an aggressive playstyle to instantly melt enemies using the double-shot enhanced basic attack at point-blank range and the Passive's penetration stacks.
hero_057.lateGame: The existence of Skill 3 is your lifeline. Upon casting, it instantly removes any CC on yourself and makes you completely immune to CC for 3 seconds. Simultaneously, you gain a massive shield and a physical attack boost, and can continuously convert 60% of the damage dealt to enemy heroes into shield value. This allows you to rampage on the frontline, demonstrating unmatched durability by continuously dealing damage while ignoring enemy control skills.
hero_057.teamfight: In teamfights, to maximize the CC immunity and shield conversion effects of Skill 3, activate it the moment enemy CC skills fly in to cleanse and negate them, and carve your way deep into the enemy formation. Knock back troublesome enemy heroes with Skill 2 to catch them, deal damage with Skill 1's double shot and basic attacks at point-blank range, and collapse the enemy formation while recovering your own shield.
hero_057.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is trying to trade blows from a distance, as the damage from the Passive's shotgun drops drastically when farther away since the outer bullets won't hit. Also, it's common to waste the powerful Skill 3 at the start of a fight when the shield is full, only to be unable to cleanse when hit by a fatal enemy CC later, resulting in death. Failing to hit enemy heroes with Skill 2's dash and losing the CD reduction effect also leads to a drop in burst damage.
hero_057.strengths: The greatest strength is the overwhelming close-quarters combat capability derived from the burst damage when at point-blank range and the physical armor penetration stacks. Moreover, with the CC cleanse and 3-second CC immunity from Skill 3, combined with the effect of converting damage dealt into a shield, you possess astonishing tenacity to forcefully continue attacking while ignoring enemy disruption. The jungle clear speed is also very fast, providing a kit that easily snowballs from the early game.
hero_057.weaknesses: Since the means of attack relies on basic attacks (buckshot) and requires being completely point-blank to deal damage, if continually kited by movement speed reductions or skills that prevent approaches, you can't do anything. The shield from Skill 3 recovers by dealing damage, but if the shield is broken and CC immunity drops, or if subjected to effects like blind, you will instantly expose your fragility and be killed. Lacking mobility skills to cross walls is also a weakness.

hero_058.earlyGame: The top priority is to quickly build up the 'Madness' gauge required to cast Transformation (Skill 3). Since every basic attack generates 3 Madness, actively attack minions and monsters. Knock back enemy heroes with the sweep of Skill 1, earning Madness based on the number of enemies hit. When an ally is about to take damage, jump to a designated location with Skill 2, providing a shield to allies in the area to efficiently build Madness.
hero_058.midGame: Once Madness is fully charged, aim to initiate teamfights using the powerful Skill 3. Casting Skill 3 unleashes a roar, blowing enemies away and stunning those at the end of the trajectory for 1.5 seconds. During this roar, you are invincible and CC immune for 1.25 seconds, and gain a massive shield equal to 40% of max HP, enabling forceful dives under enemy towers or fierce counterattacks from disadvantageous situations.
hero_058.lateGame: Utilize the high disruption capability and burst damage in Demon Mode (after casting Skill 3). Basic attacks during Demon Mode deal additional physical damage, and casting skills adds a Passive effect that shakes the ground, reducing enemy movement speed by 25% for 2 seconds. Position yourself on the frontline using the massive shield, and dash along the roar's trajectory with a 50% movement speed increase, disrupting the enemy formation and creating attack opportunities for allies.
hero_058.teamfight: Support allies by providing shields (Skill 2) while looking for an opportunity, and unleash Skill 3's roar the moment enemies group up or allies are attacked. Ideally, split the enemy formation and neutralize multiple enemies with the stun effect at the end. After transforming, act as a frontline tank absorbing damage with massive durability, and stick to the enemy carries to severely restrict their actions by scattering enhanced basic attacks and movement speed reduction effects.
hero_058.commonMistakes: The biggest mistake is failing to contribute anything right before a teamfight because Madness is lacking, as the most powerful Skill 3 cannot be cast at all without the gauge filled. Also, aiming the roar of Skill 3 in the wrong direction can save a low-HP enemy an ally was trying to kill, or fatally blast an enemy assassin right in front of your backline, assisting in their kill.
hero_058.strengths: The overall team and self-durability are exceptionally high due to the massive shield equal to 40% of max HP from Skill 3, and the ability to grant shields to allies with Skill 2. The wide-area knockback and stun of Skill 3 have the power to instantly turn the tide, adaptable from engaging to disengaging in combat. While transformed, burst damage and disruption capabilities also increase, projecting a presence far beyond a simple wall.
hero_058.weaknesses: Since all strengths rely on 'charging Madness to transform with Skill 3,' you are merely an entity that hands out shields until Madness is built. If enemies continuously force fights without giving you the opening to charge Madness, you will be overwhelmed without being able to use Skill 3. Furthermore, after transforming, the effect of Skill 2 granting shields to allies is lost, reducing your support capability to directly protect teammates.

hero_059.earlyGame: Fight while keeping the Passive's five-weapon switching system in mind. Capitalize on the trait where every basic attack hit recovers your own HP and gradually switches to weapons with longer range, maintaining sustain during the laning phase. Landing attacks and skills builds Fighting Spirit stacks to increase attack speed, so maintain stacks by attacking minions, and fight advantageously using Skill 1's dash and knock-up in Guard Mode, and Skill 2's thrust and healing.
hero_059.midGame: Understand the modes (Guard, Combat, Overkill) that change as weapons switch. Particularly, entering 'Overkill Mode' with the fifth weapon, the bow, reduces the cooldowns of Skills 1 and 2 by 50%, allowing you to frequently cycle skills from a distance to output damage. When a fight is imminent, attack minions in advance to build Fighting Spirit stacks and advance the weapon stages, creating an advantage in range and attack speed before engaging.
hero_059.lateGame: The timing of Skill 3 (Ultimate) dictates everything. Upon casting, not only do you gain a massive 2-second movement speed boost and 35% attack speed increase, but for 6 seconds the Fighting Spirit stack limit is removed, and basic attacks deal additional damage. Moreover, since you can basic attack while moving, dodge enemy attacks while unleashing an overwhelming barrage of long-range attacks, acting as the ultimate carry capable of single-handedly collapsing the enemy team.
hero_059.teamfight: Increase weapon range with basic attacks and initiate attacks from the safety of the backline. Cast Skill 3 the moment key enemies step forward or allies initiate the fight. While infinitely stacking Fighting Spirit, kite to deny enemy approaches, and utilize the shortened CDs of Overkill Mode to lay down a barrage of skills and basic attacks. If your HP drops, use the multi-hit healing of Skill 2 to sustain and stay on the frontline.
hero_059.commonMistakes: A fatal mistake is not knowing which weapon you currently hold, diving into the enemy while in the melee Guard Mode thinking you are ranged, or conversely getting defenselessly close while in the ranged Overkill Mode. Also, failing to build any Fighting Spirit stacks before casting Skill 3, leading to the effect expiring before attack speed maxes out, or standing still to trade hits without utilizing the strength of attacking while moving, wastes their potential.
hero_059.strengths: The greatest strength is the ability to 'basic attack while moving' when Skill 3 is active, and the overwhelming sustained burst damage from the uncapped attack speed increase. Kiting capabilities are exceptionally high, displaying unmatched strength against melee heroes. Also, possessing HP recovery means via basic attacks and Skill 2 grants high survivability, and the longer the fight drags on, the more Fighting Spirit stacks, growing into an unstoppable damage dealer.
hero_059.weaknesses: Since you cannot freely switch weapons and can only advance stages by landing basic attacks, you are vulnerable to being counterattacked due to short range at the very start of a fight (especially when the weapon is short). Moreover, while the powerful buff from Skill 3 is down, mobility and burst damage drop significantly, leaving you helpless if an enemy assassin instantly closes the gap. Complex Passive management and high operational precision to keep moving while attacking are constantly required.

hero_060.earlyGame: Since the Passive allows you to deal constant additional damage to monsters, your jungle and neutral monster clear speed is exceptionally high, gaining an early farm advantage. Apply Skill 1 to enemy heroes to increase attack speed and range, and deal massive damage by detonating it instantly after landing 4 basic attacks. The Passive's eavesdrop effect grants 1 second of vision on unseen enemy heroes, allowing you to prevent ambushes and position safely.
hero_060.midGame: Utilize your high objective-securing ability to bring advantageous buffs to the team. The mark damage from Skill 1 is also effective on towers, so once you force enemies out of lane, quickly destroy the tower to expand map control. Since you become untargetable during Skill 2's dash, use it as a counter to dodge powerful CC or fatal damage skills from the enemy, and use the remaining burning area to restrict enemy movement and give chase.
hero_060.lateGame: Deploy Skill 3's massive tornado at a designated location to control the teamfight area. It deals powerful continuous damage and a 50% movement speed reduction to enemies within range, with damage doubled for those in the center. Since the tornado's damage also builds Skill 1's mark stacks, applying Skill 1 to an enemy inside the tornado will instantly trigger a 4-stack detonation. Preserve Skill 2's dash to ensure survival against enemy assaults.
hero_060.teamfight: Place Skill 3's tornado where enemies group up or on their retreat paths to split the formation, scattering movement speed reductions and massive damage over a wide area. Apply Skill 1 to the highest priority enemy carry or tank, and aim for a reliable kill by quickly detonating the 4 stacks using the tornado's damage and your own basic attacks. Against enemy approaches or fatal attacks, use the untargetable dash of Skill 2 to dodge precisely, and continue outputting high sustained damage from a safe distance.
hero_060.commonMistakes: Skill 2 is an excellent evasion skill, but the most common mistake is using it carelessly just to approach or damage enemies, only to be killed unable to dodge the fatal enemy skills that follow immediately after. Furthermore, failing to land 4 basic attacks after applying Skill 1's mark, allowing the enemy to escape without dealing the maximum detonation damage, is another common failure leading directly to insufficient burst damage.
hero_060.strengths: The greatest strength is having one of the fastest objective (jungle, dragon, tower) clear speeds among all heroes, thanks to the extra monster damage from the Passive and the mark detonation of Skill 1. Also, despite being a marksman, they possess a powerful self-defense tool in the untargetable movement of Skill 2, making them highly survivable combined with the vision-granting ability of the Passive. Possesses wide-area zoning capability with Skill 3, boasting excellent performance in both offense and defense.
hero_060.weaknesses: Since a large portion of damage relies on the detonation damage of Skill 1, having the mark cleansed by Purify (CC cleanse spell) or specific hero debuff cleansing skills drastically reduces burst damage. Also, compared to other pure basic-attack-focused marksmen, sustained damage solely from basic attacks in the very late game is somewhat inferior. The vulnerability after consuming Skill 2 is large, and being targeted by multiple enemies there makes them very fragile.
